medical_history.chief_complaint: Bloom syndrome, a rare autosomal recessive condition characterised by chromosomal instability leading to a high risk of cancer at an early age
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Diagnosis of Bloom syndrome was made in 1978, confirmed by significantly increased sister chromatid exchanges on chromosome examination. She has had a long-standing photosensitive rash on her face. She developed her first cancer at the age of 38 years - a basal cell carcinoma on her right upper lip. Her second cancer was found on screening colonoscopy at the age of 39 years.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Type 2 diabetes mellitus, fibroadenoma of the left breast, raised cholesterol, osteoporosis, intermittent viral labyrinthitis, cirrhosis of the liver, left-sided pelvic kidney, probable bilateral small renal angiomyolipomata, total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy at the age of 35 years.
medical_history.family_history: Non-consanguineous Ashkenazi Jewish parents. Her mother, maternal aunt, grandmother, and great-grandmother shared the feature of developing very few secondary teeth.
medical_history.social_history: Routinely uses high factor sunscreens. Has taken regular vitamin C and multivitamin supplements from her mid-twenties, and more recently supplements of vitamins A, C, and E for their antioxidant properties. Has had influenza and pneumococcal vaccinations.

physical_examination.general_examination: Photosensitive rash on her face, several cafe au lait macules in the gluteal region, final adult height is 129 cm, decreased subcutaneous tissue, dysmorphic features including a beaked nose and micrognathia, dystrophic nails.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Fluctuating liver enzymes over many years

radiographic_examinations.findings: Left-sided pelvic kidney and probable bilateral small renal angiomyolipomata

radiographic_examinations.procedure_name: Computed tomography (CT) of chest, abdomen, and pelvis

other_tests.findings: Six 2-3 mm polyps removed from the transverse colon with histology showing tubular adenomas with low-grade dysplasia. Later, a 10 cm pedunculated polyp excised from the sigmoid colon with a focus of intramucosal carcinoma
other_tests.changes_over_time: Initial findings showed small polyps, later findings showed a larger polyp with intramucosal carcinoma

other_tests.findings: Basal cell carcinoma on the right upper lip, excised successfully

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was confirmed by finding significantly increased sister chromatid exchanges on chromosome examination. The patient exhibited key clinical features including growth retardation, dysmorphic features, a photosensitive rash on the face, and a high risk of cancer at an early age.
